calendar.matters:
Petition Of Antoinette Mallette And Veronica Ligne Mar For Surcharge Of Gregory Mars Distributive Share Of Trust Property And Award Of Attorney Fees From Trust, Probate Code 850(A)(3), 17200
Off Calendar Oct-09-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Spalding for petitioners Antoinette Mallette and Veronica Ligne Mar, who were also present.
Thomas Drohan for respondent Gregory Mar.
Christina Nalchajian for Independent Special Trustee Jennifer Winship.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Surcharge of Gregory Mar's Distributive Share of Trust Property and Award of Attorney Fees from Trust.

The Court noted that during the April 2024 trial, all parties stipulated to waive claims for attorney's fees against one another and agreed no fee award would issue, which appeared dispositive of the current petition.

Mr. Spalding argued the stipulation did not cover conduct occurring after trial, and alleged Gregory Mar violated court orders by remaining in the trust property for an additional year, causing further expenses and attorney's fees.

Ms. Nalchajian stated that her client is neutral, had not yet been compensated due to lack of liquidity, and anticipated filing a petition for instructions regarding distribution once the property was sold. She clarified that her client's authority was limited to sale of the property and payment of necessary expenses from escrow.

Mr. Drohan argued that the waiver of attorney's fees remained binding and that petitioners lacked standing to request fees on behalf of the trustee. He acknowledged some increased costs due to Gregory Mar's conduct but maintained that any trustee compensation or expense allocation should be addressed separately in a petition for instructions.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to deny the petition.

Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Spaulding, Esq. appearing by the Department 204 telephone (due to Zoom issues this afternoon) for Respondents Antoinette Mallette and Veronica Ligne Mar.
Tom Drohan, Esq. of Legal Assistance for the Elderly appearing in person in Dept. 204 for Gregory Mar.
Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq. appearing by Zoom as Co-Counsel for Gregory Mar.

Matter called. ARGUMENT on the Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Antoinette Mallette And Veronica Ligne Mar For Summary Adjudication Of First Cause Of Action For Modification Of Trust is HELD.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with the following modification as to the evidentiary objections:

The Court grants both of Respondents' Requests for Judicial Notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (21015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.)

Respondents' Evidentiary Objection No. 1 is overruled.
Respondents' Evidentiary Objection No. 2 is overruled.
Respondents' Evidentiary Objection No. 3 is overruled.
Respondents' Evidentiary Objection No. 4 is overruled.
Respondents' Evidentiary Objection No. 5 is overruled.

Respondents' Motion for Summary Adjudication on the First Cause of Action to the Petition to Modify Trust and Compel Co-Trustees to Account, to Remove Co-Trustees, to Declare Deeds Void; for Redress from Breaches of Trust; to Enjoin Co-Trustees is denied.

In support of the motion, Respondents rely primarily on Petitioner's discovery responses made prior to the October 5, 2020 court orders granting Respondents' motions to compel further responses. The Court granted these motions to compel further answers on a finding that the responses were insufficient. Petitioner's opposition to this motion relies primarily on his discovery responses made after the October 5, 2020 orders, as well as a declaration by the drafting attorney. Respondents urge the Court to disregard both, citing Whitmire v. Ingersoll-Rand Co. (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 1078 and lodging evidentiary objections. The circumstances here are not similar to Whitmire. In Whitmire, the relied-upon declaration contradicted responses produced in discovery from the same party. Here, the declarations submitted in support of the opposition to the motion do not contradict Petitioner's discovery responses produced after the October 5, 2020 orders compelling further responses to interrogatories and requests for admission.

Further, although a moving party may establish its prima facie entitlement to summary adjudication by demonstrating that an opponent's discovery responses are devoid of supportive evidence, merely evasive or incomplete answers to written discovery are not legally sufficient grounds for granting a motion for summary judgment or adjudication when other evidence presented on the motion shows there are triable issues of material fact. (See Ahn v. Kumho Tire U.S.A., Inc. (2014) 166 Cal.Rptr.3d 852.) Here, the evidence presented in support of and in opposition to the motion for summary adjudication of the Petition's First Cause of Action shows there are triable issues of material fact regarding the settlor's knowledge, awareness, and intentions in establishing the trust at the time the instrument was drafted and executed.

Mr. Drohan stated that he will prepare a form of order that adopts the tentative ruling and reflects the Court's modification today in open court as to the evidentiary objections. Mr. Drohan stated that he will submit the proposed order to Mr. Spaulding before submitting it to the Court for review and signature.

The motion is denied in part as to the argument that previously-produced documents were not in conformity with current CCP section 2031.280; the current statute became applicable after responses to the RFAs were produced and after this motion was filed, and there is no showing that the documents that were produced in 2019 were not in compliance with the prior statute. The motion is also denied in part as to RFAs 14 and 15, as the disqualification claim has been decided and discovery on that issue is now not relevant. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Petitoner is ordered to serve further responses to RFA's, Set One in compliance with this order by October 7, 2020.
